questions.q: The Dadasaheb Phalke Award is named after Dhundiraj Govind Phalke, who is widely regarded as the father of which national cinema?
questions.explanation: The award honours Dhundiraj Govind Phalke, popularly known as Dadasaheb Phalke, who is regarded as the father of Indian cinema.

questions.q: In which year was the Dadasaheb Phalke Award first introduced by the Government of India?
questions.explanation: The Dadasaheb Phalke Award was introduced in 1969 to honour lifetime contribution to Indian cinema.

questions.q: Who was the first recipient of the Dadasaheb Phalke Award?
questions.explanation: Actress Devika Rani became the first recipient of the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 1969.

questions.q: The Dadasaheb Phalke Award recognises outstanding contribution of what kind to Indian cinema?
questions.explanation: The Dadasaheb Phalke Award is given for lifetime contribution to the growth and development of Indian cinema.

questions.q: Dadasaheb Phalke, after whom the award is named, directed which film in 1913, regarded as India's first full-length feature?
questions.explanation: Dadasaheb Phalke directed Raja Harishchandra in 1913, considered India's first full-length feature film.

questions.q: The Dadasaheb Phalke Award is presented as part of which annual ceremony in India?
questions.explanation: The Dadasaheb Phalke Award is conferred as part of the National Film Awards ceremony.

questions.q: Which government ministry is responsible for conferring the Dadasaheb Phalke Award?
questions.explanation: The Dadasaheb Phalke Award is conferred by the Government of India through the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting.

questions.q: Legendary playback singer Lata Mangeshkar received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 1989. In which field of cinema did she earn her fame?
questions.explanation: Lata Mangeshkar, honoured with the award in 1989, was celebrated as one of India's greatest playback singers.

questions.q: Satyajit Ray, who received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 1984, was primarily celebrated as a master in which role?
questions.explanation: Satyajit Ray, honoured in 1984, was acclaimed as one of the greatest film directors in world cinema.

questions.q: The Dadasaheb Phalke Award is described as India's highest honour in which field?
questions.explanation: The Dadasaheb Phalke Award is India's highest award in the field of cinema.

questions.q: Which actor became the first male recipient of the Dadasaheb Phalke Award, honoured for 1971?
questions.explanation: Prithviraj Kapoor was the first male recipient, awarded the honour posthumously for 1971.

questions.q: The Dadasaheb Phalke Award medallion prominently features which symbol associated with the honour?
questions.explanation: The Dadasaheb Phalke Award carries the Swarna Kamal (Golden Lotus) medallion along with a cash prize and shawl.

questions.q: Which Assamese singer, composer and filmmaker received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 1992?
questions.explanation: Bhupen Hazarika, the celebrated Assamese singer-composer and filmmaker, was honoured with the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 1992.

questions.q: Veteran actor Dilip Kumar received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in which year?
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar was conferred the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 1994.

questions.q: Which legendary showman, actor and filmmaker of the Kapoor family received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 1987?
questions.explanation: Raj Kapoor, the celebrated showman of Indian cinema, received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 1987.

questions.q: Pioneering Kannada actor and producer Dr. Rajkumar received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award, recognising his contribution chiefly to which film industry?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajkumar was honoured for his towering contribution to Kannada cinema.

questions.q: Which singer, sister of Lata Mangeshkar, received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award herself in the year 2000?
questions.explanation: Asha Bhosle received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 2000, years after her sister Lata Mangeshkar was honoured.

questions.q: Telugu superstar and politician N. T. Rama Rao received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award primarily for his work in which language cinema?
questions.explanation: N. T. Rama Rao was recognised for his iconic contribution to Telugu cinema.

questions.q: Which Hindi cinema icon, famed as the 'Angry Young Man' of the 1970s, was honoured with the Dadasaheb Phalke Award for his lifetime contribution?
questions.explanation: Amitabh Bachchan, the legendary 'Angry Young Man' of 1970s Hindi cinema, was conferred the Dadasaheb Phalke Award for his lifetime contribution to Indian cinema.

questions.q: Which veteran actress, celebrated for Guru Dutt classics like 'Pyaasa' and 'Kaagaz Ke Phool' and for 'Guide', was honoured with the Dadasaheb Phalke Award for the year 2021?
questions.explanation: Waheeda Rehman, the acclaimed actress of Guru Dutt-era classics and 'Guide', was honoured with the Dadasaheb Phalke Award for the year 2021.

questions.q: Yash Chopra, the filmmaker behind many iconic Hindi romances, received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in which year?
questions.explanation: Yash Chopra was conferred the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 2001.

questions.q: Filmmaker Mrinal Sen, a leading figure of Indian parallel cinema, received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in which year?
questions.explanation: Mrinal Sen, a pioneer of Indian art cinema, was conferred the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 2003.

questions.q: Tamil filmmaker K. Balachander, mentor to many leading stars, received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award honouring his work for which year?
questions.explanation: K. Balachander received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award for the year 2010.

questions.q: Which renowned Malayalam director and screenwriter received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award for the year 2004?
questions.explanation: Adoor Gopalakrishnan, a master of Malayalam parallel cinema, received the award for 2004.

questions.q: Filmmaker and actor V. Shantaram, a pioneer of socially conscious Indian cinema, received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in which year?
questions.explanation: V. Shantaram was conferred the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 1985, recognising his pioneering work.

questions.q: Which pioneering film producer, founder of the New Theatres studio in Calcutta, received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 1970 as its second-ever recipient?
questions.explanation: Birendranath (B. N.) Sircar, founder of New Theatres in Calcutta, received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 1970, becoming its second recipient after Devika Rani.

questions.q: Which silent-era actress, once among the highest-paid stars of Indian cinema, received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 1973?
questions.explanation: Ruby Myers, known by her screen name Sulochana, received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 1973.

questions.q: Bengali actress and singer Kanan Devi, a major star of New Theatres, received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in which year?
questions.explanation: Kanan Devi was conferred the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 1976.

questions.q: Which veteran producer-director, a major figure across Hindi, Telugu and Tamil cinema with hits like 'Sasural' and 'Milan', received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 1982?
questions.explanation: Producer-director L. V. Prasad, a major figure across Hindi, Telugu and Tamil cinema, received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 1982.

questions.q: Veteran filmmaker and screenwriter Hrishikesh Mukherjee, known for films like 'Anand' and 'Gol Maal', received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in which year?
questions.explanation: Hrishikesh Mukherjee was conferred the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 1999.
